text:
There once was a young boy named Milo who lived in the big city. He loved flying kites. Every evening after school, Milo would head out to a park overlooking the serene lake. He'd throw his brightly colored kite into the sky until it disappeared amongst the twinkling stars.

One day, Milo's kite got a life of its own. It flew higher and higher until it became a star-shaped lantern bobbing cheerfully high. Each night, Milo could listen to its echo singing melodies.

A few moonlit nights later, something extraordinary happened. Milo noticed that his kite seemed to have an intelligence of its own. It danced around the park, twirling through clouds, leaving glistening trails of glittering droplets.

Milo befriended the kite, who turned out to be a celestial messenger from the stars. The kite told Milo stories about the galaxy, shared dreams of flying through nebulae and shining like a million stars.
